steps.title: Check the median
steps.content:
Let the original weights be listed from least to greatest.
  • With $14$ weights, the original median is the average of the $7$th and $8$th values.
  • The added value, $85$, is larger than the original weights shown, so it goes at the end of the list.
  • With $15$ weights, the new median is the $8$th original value.
The median would increase only if the $8$th value is greater than the $7$th value. The histogram does not force that, so statement I is not guaranteed.

steps.title: Check the mean
steps.content: The added value, $85$, is greater than every original weight shown in the histogram. A value greater than all original values is greater than the original mean, so adding it must increase the mean. Statement II is guaranteed.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: Only statement II must be true, so choice B is correct.
